title: Cacio e Pepe
intro:
Cacio e pepe is one of Rome’s most elemental pasta dishes. Shepherds in Lazio carried dried pasta, black pepper, and aged sheep’s milk cheese because they traveled well. When pasta is tossed with pepper, cheese, and starchy pasta water, the mixture emulsifies into a creamy sauce without cream or butter.

This version keeps the Roman technique but replaces most of the cheese with a blended sauce of caramelized onions, white beans, and a small amount of white miso. The onions provide deep browned flavor, the beans create body and emulsification, and the miso supplies fermented sharpness. A small sprinkle of grated “shaky” Parmesan-style cheese at the end adds a familiar aged-cheese aroma without letting cheese dominate the dish.

stepGroups.title: Caramelize the onions
items: Heat a wide heavy skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the olive oil, sliced onion, and salt and spread into an even layer.
Do not stir for about 45 to 60 minutes. The onions will release liquid and cook in their own juices, softening and concentrating without burning.
Once the liquid has mostly evaporated and the onions begin to sizzle, start stirring every few minutes and scraping the pan to reincorporate browned bits.

Continue cooking until the onions are deep brown, jammy, and very soft, about another 45 to 60 minutes (roughly 1½ to 2 hours total). If sticking becomes aggressive, add a tablespoon of water and scrape the pan clean.

stepGroups.title: Cook the pasta
items: Bring the water to a boil in a large pot. Add the kosher salt and pasta. Cook until just barely al dente.
Before draining, reserve at least 1 cup of the pasta cooking water.

stepGroups.title: Blend the sauce
items: Transfer the caramelized onions to a blender. Add the white beans, white miso, and about ¾ cup of the hot pasta water.
Blend until completely smooth. The sauce should be about the consistency of light cream.

stepGroups.title: Bloom the pepper
items: Place a wide skillet over medium heat. Add the olive oil and the crushed pepper. Cook for about 30 seconds until very fragrant.
Add about 2 tablespoons of pasta water and let it simmer briefly. The starch helps distribute the pepper flavor through the sauce.

stepGroups.title: Combine pasta and sauce
items: Transfer the cooked pasta directly into the pepper skillet.
Add the blended onion-bean sauce and toss continuously. Add small splashes of pasta water as needed while tossing until the sauce becomes glossy and coats the noodles.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Remove the pan from the heat. Add the lemon juice and toss once more.
Plate the pasta and finish with a light sprinkle of the grated Parmesan-style cheese.
